title: Error: wrong number of file descriptors for socket activation protocol (2 != 1)
body:
### Issue Description
See also https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/739597/podman-error-wrong-number-of-file-descriptors-for-socket-activation-protocol-2

When trying to configure an alternative (rootful) socket(to replicate the standard `/var/run/docker.sock` path expected by docker-compatible tools), podman systemd socket activation entrypoint fails complaining about multiple file descriptors.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a unit file for a docker socket:
```
# /usr/lib/systemd/system/podman.socket
[Unit]
Description=Docker API Socket
Documentation=man:podman-system-service(1)

[Socket]
ListenStream=%t/docker.sock
SocketMode=0660
Service=podman.service

[Install]
WantedBy=sockets.target

[Socket]
SocketGroup=wheel
```
3. override podman service to rely on docker socket
```
# /etc/systemd/system/podman.service.d/override.conf
[Unit]
Requires=
After=
Requires=docker.socket
After=docker.socket
```
4. start/enable: `systemctl start docker.socket`
5. trigger socket activation: `nc -U /run/docker.sock`


### Describe the results you received

podman service loop-failing at startup:
```
Mar 10 16:00:01 drpyser-wazo-workstation systemd[1]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Mar 10 16:00:01 drpyser-wazo-workstation systemd[1]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Mar 10 16:00:01 drpyser-wazo-workstation systemd[1]: Starting Podman API Service...
Mar 10 16:00:01 drpyser-wazo-workstation podman[317022]: time="2023-03-10T16:00:01-05:00" level=info msg="/usr/bin/podman filtering at log level info"
Mar 10 16:00:01 drpyser-wazo-workstation podman[317022]: time="2023-03-10T16:00:01-05:00" level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 49"
Mar 10 16:00:01 drpyser-wazo-workstation podman[317022]: time="2023-03-10T16:00:01-05:00" level=info msg="Using systemd socket activation to determine API endpoint"
Mar 10 16:00:01 drpyser-wazo-workstation podman[317022]: Error: wrong number of file descriptors for socket activation protocol (2 != 1)
Mar 10 16:00:02 drpyser-wazo-workstation systemd[1]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Mar 10 16:00:02 drpyser-wazo-workstation systemd[1]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Mar 10 16:00:02 drpyser-wazo-workstation systemd[1]: Failed to start Podman API Service.
```


### Describe the results you expected

* podman should be able to respond to activations from multiple sockets?
* it should be possible to ignore the `podman.socket` and set an alternative socket, or otherwise change the default socket path


### podman info output

```yaml

  cpuUtilization:
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

archlinux system:
`Linux drpyser-wazo-workstation 6.1.14-1-lts #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 25 Feb 2023 11:02:38 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux`

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I don't see why the podman service should be able listen on two sockets at the same time. This would just complicate things, why not just symlink the paths or spawn two podman services?
I agree, we don't want to support two sockets at the same time.
